title: The Log Begins
reflection: Established the evolution log system — a persistent thinking journal that Claude reads at session start and writes to at session end. This is the context engineering that makes autonomous daily improvement possible.
looking_ahead: Make temperament visible through color
Sound envelopes for bond formation/breaking
Event permanence: meteor craters, flood channels

title: Temperament Made Visible
reflection: Motes had three temperament axes computed at birth but rendered as a single energy-colored pixel. Replaced the 3-level color lookup with a continuous hue function: wanderlust shifts warm, sociability shifts cool, hardiness resists dimming at low energy.
looking_ahead: Sound envelopes for musical bond moments
Activate spatial hash grid for performance
Event permanence: terrain marks that persist through the cycle

title: Bonds as Relationships
reflection: Bond lines were a single flat biome-dim color for every pair. Now they blend the temperament colors of their two endpoints, making cluster character visible. Pre-computed all mote colors into a Map for efficiency.
looking_ahead: Sound envelopes for bond formation chimes
Activate spatial hash grid
Event permanence: crater tiles, waterlogged terrain

title: Making the Invisible Visible
reflection: Rich simulation was running but invisible at the pixel level. Motes grew from 1px to 5px crosses (3x3 when bonded), bonds became visible at alpha 100, clicks produce ripple rings, meteor got a trail and impact flash, and the spatial hash grid was wired up for O(1) neighbor queries.
looking_ahead: Sound envelopes for bond formation
Death particles on mote expiration
Settlement markers with structural shapes

title: The Sky Remembers the Hour
reflection: The sky was phase-blind — same blue from genesis to silence. Added skyTintAt() that interpolates per-phase RGB tints across the cycle: cool violet at genesis, neutral midday, amber at dissolution, indigo at silence. Strongest at zenith, fading to 30% at horizon.
looking_ahead: Death particles for dissolution visibility
Sound envelopes for bond chimes
Mote age visibility: gold tinge for elders

title: The World Comes Alive
reflection: Comprehensive visual overhaul: breathing mote pulse, cluster glow auras, soul-rise death particles, dramatic eclipse with stars and glowing eyes, aurora curtains, enhanced meteor trail, audio prompt overlay, and ambient narrative text. Rewrote daily-evolve.md from conservative to ambitious.
looking_ahead: WebGL post-processing for real bloom/glow
Creature memory: motes returning to meaningful places
Visible trails and history accumulating through the cycle
Sound evolution: phase transitions with musical moments

title: Architecture Refactor: Clean DAG, Focused Files
reflection: Restructured from 15 to 25 files with a clean layered DAG. Split the 922-line main.ts god file into 6 focused modules (narrative, render-motes, render-effects, render-bonds, render-ui, thin orchestrator). Extracted config.ts/types.ts foundation, split terrain and weather into data/render pairs, fixed circular deps, cached clusters.
looking_ahead: WebGL post-processing for bloom/glow
Creature memory and meaningful return behaviors
Sound evolution: phase transitions with musical moments

title: Death Made Dramatic
reflection: Death was the weakest visual moment in the cycle — a handful of generic biome-colored pixels that vanished in 1.2 seconds. Two bugs combined to kill the effect: deaths captured the landscape's palette color instead of the mote's temperament color, and the particle system was too sparse to notice. Fixed the color capture in world.ts to compute the actual hue/saturation from temperament, then rewrote renderDeathParticles into four distinct phases — flash burst, five soul shards arcing upward, a rising spirit orb that sways and pulses for 2.8 seconds, and a soft ground echo that lingers for 7 seconds. Now each death is a small spectacle in the mote's own identity color.
looking_ahead: Temperament movement distinction: wanderers leave longer trails, social motes pulse when bonded
Bond formation visual moment: a brief arc of light connecting two motes as they bond
Cluster identity: large clusters develop a shared color aura that marks their territory
Hardy motes: visual resilience cue when surviving hostile terrain (brief white flash)

title: Five Worlds, Five Voices
reflection: The sound system was biome-blind — desert and tundra sounded identical, every world hummed the same C3 root. Rewrote the engine around five distinct sonic identities: desert uses a bright E3 root with 3.5-second reverb and pure sine bells (heat shimmer); tundra drops to A2 with 4.5-second reverb, high filter Q, and maximum stereo width (frozen distances); volcanic grounds to E2 with short stone-surface reverb and sawtooth voices (threatening); lush sits on G2 with dense canopy reverb and rich triangle waves; temperate stays warm on C3. Each biome also gets its own scale vocabulary per phase — volcanic now builds from tritone clusters through phrygian dominant to octatonic and back to bare tritone silence. Added stereo panning per voice cluster (X position maps to stereo field), an LFO for organic filter breathing tuned per biome, perfect-fifth bond chimes routed through reverb, and a descending-glide death sound that resonates. The eclipse gets a dual-drone: deep C2 bass plus the eerie 7th harmonic. Aurora now spreads a harmonic series across the full stereo field.
looking_ahead: Spawn sound: a gentle arrival tone as each mote enters the world
Phase transition musical moment: a brief chord stab or silence at each phase boundary
Cluster fragmentation sound: when a large cluster breaks apart, a brief dissonant scatter
Biome ambient texture: add a very quiet pitched-noise bed per biome (wind harmonics for tundra, insect chorus for lush, deep earth rumble for volcanic)

title: The Sky Learns to Tell Time
reflection: The celestial system was the biggest lie in the simulation — sun and moon hung at random static positions, indifferent to the hour. A genesis world looked identical to a dissolution world except for the sky tint. Fixed this with a full cycle-driven solar arc: the sun rises from the eastern horizon at exploration (cycleProgress ~0.06), climbs through a parabolic arc to zenith at organization/complexity, then descends and reddens toward the western horizon at dissolution. Moon rises from the east as the cycle darkens toward silence. Stars fade in from 78% of cycle onward — 70 deterministic stars of three types (warm giants, blue-white, common white) that twinkle independently. Weather particles are now biome-aware: desert storms throw ochre sand streaks at 45°; volcanic overcast drops slow dark ash flakes with wider spread; tundra blizzards take on a colder blue-white tint. Fog gained biome identity — desert haze is warm tan, volcanic fog is suffocating dark ash grey, tundra fog reads icy blue. Water shimmer went from a single sparse sparkle every 5 pixels to three overlapping wave frequencies producing glints, sub-surface ripple color, and dark wave troughs. Background mountains gained per-biome color tints and a depth-based blend (up to 52% opacity near the surface, fading toward sky) making the distant ranges feel present and three-dimensional.
looking_ahead: Horizon glow: warm orange band at sky-terrain boundary during genesis and dissolution
Desert biome: heat shimmer — vertical distortion effect on lower sky near terrain
Volcanic biome: lava pools as a rare terrain feature (glowing orange deep-water tiles)
Phase-reactive terrain: water level rising during flood event, vegetation darkening in drought

title: The Cycle Learns to Tell Its Own Story
reflection: The narrative was the weakest layer — viewers watched 5 minutes of rich simulation accompanied by 6-7 generic text fragments with no emotional arc. Every phase sounded the same. The rare event, if it happened, fired with no warning and no echo. Rewrote narrative.ts around a proper story structure: each of the 6 phases now triggers a deterministic opening line picked from a pool of 4 options, so every cycle's arc sounds distinct. Genesis reads the weather and opens with 'rain falls on a waking world' or 'fog shrouds the first breath' when the atmosphere warrants it. An anticipation window fires ~25 seconds before each rare event — 'the air shifts', 'the world holds its breath' — building tension before anything visible happens. When the event ends, an aftermath line follows ('the scar endures', 'the shadow remembers') giving each spectacle a reflection. Also fixed the event trigger point: all 9 event types previously fired at 45% of the cycle; now each has its own position — aurora fires late (70%) as a prelude to dissolution, drought fires early (38%) for maximum suffering, meteor peaks at 60% when the most witnesses are alive. Migration was a ping-pong — motes rushed right then immediately reversed; now it's a sustained single-direction wave seeded from the cycle, so it feels like collective will rather than a physics glitch. Event canvas messages got a pass too: 'SOMETHING FALLS' became 'FIRE FROM ABOVE', 'AN ECLIPSE DESCENDS' became 'DARKNESS DESCENDS'. Expanded the cycle name vocabulary from 30+15 to 45+25 words, adding FORSAKEN, WANDERING, TWILIGHT, THRESHOLD, EDGE and others.
looking_ahead: Bond formation flash: a brief arc of light between two motes as they bond for the first time
Dissolution dirge: a narrative beat for the first death during dissolution phase — 'the first farewell'
Event cascade narrative: when event is still active at phase transition, acknowledge the overlap
Cluster identity names: large clusters earn a name ('the northern circle', 'the bright cluster') that persists through the cycle
